summary: This source is a brief publisher's blurb for Anthony Gottlieb's book on Socrates. It highlights Socrates as the founding father of Western philosophy, noting his core views on education as self-discovery and philosophy as a practical preparation for life.

principles.statement: Education is a process of self-discovery.
principles.rationale: Socrates viewed learning not as the mere transmission of facts, but as an internal journey to discover truths for oneself.
principles.source_quote: his sense of education as self-discovery

principles.statement: Philosophy is a preparation for life.
principles.rationale: Philosophy is not merely an abstract academic exercise, but a practical discipline meant to guide how one lives and embodies their values.
principles.source_quote: his view of philosophy as preparation for life

signature_quotes.text: If you put me to death, you will not easily find anyone to take my place.
signature_quotes.context: Socrates warning his Athenian judges before being sentenced to drink hemlock.
